output_text: Photosynthesis is the process where plants convert light energy into chemical energy. The primary inputs are carbon dioxide ($\text{CO}_2$) from the air and water ($\text{H}_2\text{O}$) absorbed through the roots. This reaction takes place mainly within the chloroplasts of plant cells, utilizing chlorophyll to capture sunlight. The outputs are glucose ($\text{C}_6\text{H}_{12}\text{O}_6$), a sugar the plant uses for energy, and oxygen ($\text{O}_2$) which is released into the atmosphere.
label: row1_gemma4_e4b_mlx_vlm_bf16
prompt: Describe the process of photosynthesis at a high-school level. Cover the inputs, outputs, and where the reaction takes place. Keep your answer to roughly four sentences.
